title: Medication rash documentation

red_flags.text: Supports structured recording of drug name, reaction signs, symptoms, severity, date and time, indication, number of doses or days before onset, route, status, and specialist-investigation results already available.
red_flags.rationale: The structured documentation section supports recording already available drug and reaction details, prior records and investigation results; it does not stop a medicine or create avoidance, treatment, referral, or emergency instructions.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports structured recording of drug name, reaction signs, symptoms, severity, date and time, indication, number of doses or days before onset, route, status, and specialist-investigation results already available.

source.title: Drug allergy: diagnosis and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.9 — recording and sharing drug-allergy information

examination.text: Supports documenting history, examination, timing after exposure, erythema, urticaria, angioedema, respiratory or cardiovascular features, macules, papules, fixed eruption, fever, lymphadenopathy, mucosal erosion, vesicles, blistering, detachment, pustules, photosensitivity, and vasculitic features without asserting a diagnosis.
examination.rationale: The exact NICE assessment section supports documenting a possible medication-associated rash, onset relative to exposure, associated systemic or mucosal features, distribution and clinical examination without asserting drug allergy or causality.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting history, examination, timing after exposure, erythema, urticaria, angioedema, respiratory or cardiovascular features, macules, papules, fixed eruption, fever, lymphadenopathy, mucosal erosion, vesicles, blistering, detachment, pustules, photosensitivity, and vasculitic features without asserting a diagnosis.

source.title: Drug allergy: diagnosis and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.1.1 and Tables 1–3 — assessment, reaction patterns, and timing

sources_used.title: Drug allergy: diagnosis and management — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE allergy, emergency, medicine, and referral adaptation

source_limitations: Exact drug, formulation, dose, route, indication, exposure count, prior exposure, reaction time, morphology, mucosal and organ involvement, vital signs, diagnosis, causality, allergy status, investigations, treatment, medicine changes, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
